lines.text: Tucker here, Vilas Park perimeter, {{ELAPSED}} in. Madison-wide count is holding at 139 as of 01:58. That's 17 behind last year at this point — we're actually tracking below 2168 pace for the first time all night.

Wind's picked up hard off Mendota. Temperature's dropped to 24 degrees and I'm getting visibility maybe two blocks. Cold front moved in clean. People are moving indoors now — that's the pattern shift. The coordinated stuff stops when the weather gets this hostile.

Neighborhood breakdown: East Side still leading at 34 kills, but the rate's slowed. State Street's gone quiet — Holden, you tracking the weapons-trade data on this? Bladed work is up to 22% of total, which tracks with the ammunition pricing thing you mentioned earlier.

Yeah, and the cold actually accelerates that. Guns jam in this wind, you need a clear shot, you need time. Blade work doesn't require the setup. You're seeing economic desperation plus environmental pressure.

Vehicle-on-vehicle is down on the Beltline — I'm repositioning north toward the overpass, the cordons are holding better in this wind, people can't navigate the drag-strip routes as fast when visibility is—

tickerItems.text: MADISON RUNNING 17 BEHIND 2168 YoY | 139 cumulative at 02:00
EAST SIDE: 34 kills | STATE STREET: 19 kills (DOWN from 23:00 spike)
WIND GUSTS 22+ mph | TEMP 24°F | VISIBILITY 2 blocks | Lake Mendota chop ongoing
